description_long: This airplane features a distinctive orange and black striped livery reminiscent of a tiger pattern, covering the upper surfaces of its wings and fuselage. It is a large, multi-engine propeller aircraft with four engines mounted on a straight, mid-mounted wing. The fuselage is long and streamlined, painted primarily in light gray with the tail section also featuring the orange and black stripes. The tail assembly is a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear appears to be fixed and consists of multiple units visible beneath the wings and fuselage. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage and is enclosed, with multiple windows visible.
description: A large, multi-engine propeller airplane with orange and black tiger stripes on its wings and tail, featuring a streamlined gray fuselage and a conventional tail design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the gray fuselage, four engines on the wing, and the striped pattern on the wing and tail.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the full wingspan with orange and black stripes, the conventional tail, and the arrangement of the engines.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, showing the same features from the other side of the fuselage.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the nose, cockpit windows, all four engines, and the symmetrical striped pattern across the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines mounted on the leading edge of the wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed landing gear with multiple main and nose units visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
